docs.title: Home Builder 5
docs.text:
Home Builder is a free, open-source Blender add-on for designing interior spaces — kitchens, baths, and closets. It provides tools for drawing rooms, placing cabinets with intelligent wall snapping, and generating technical drawings.

Requires Blender 5.0+

Download Blender from blender.org.

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Draw rooms with configurable walls (exterior, interior, half walls), floors, and ceilings
  • Place cabinets & Appliances — base, upper, tall, and appliances snap to walls and fill areas in a logical way
  • Configure everything through right-click prompts: adjust cabinet dimensions, interior configurations, and construction
  • Place doors and windows that automatically cut wall openings
  • Create layout views — wall elevations, floor plans, and multi-view sheets with dimensions and annotations

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Discord Community — Ask questions and share designs
  • GitHub — Report bugs and request features
  • Contact Andrew — Direct contact

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This documentation is currently be written. For now i recommend checking out this video for a general overview of how to design with Home Builder.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Blender 5.0 or later — download here
  • Windows, macOS, or Linux

docs.title: Install from Disk
docs.text:
1. Download the latest release .zip Here
  2. Open Blender
  3. Go to Edit → Preferences → Add-ons
  4. Open the dropdown menu (top-right) and select Install from Disk...
  5. Select the downloaded .zip file

Don't extract the ZIP

Install the .zip file directly. Do not unzip it first.

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
After installing, press N in the 3D Viewport to open the sidebar. You should see a Home Builder tab.

If you see a warning about "Object Color Type is not enabled", click Open Recommended Settings to configure Blender for Home Builder.

docs.title: Installing the Extended Asset Library
docs.text:
If you want to support the development of Home Builder and get access to additional assets you can purchase the extended asset library.

  1. Purchase and Download the Extended Asset Library
  2. Open Blender
  3. Go to Edit → Preferences → Add-ons
  4. Find the Home Builder add-on.

  5. Add an external library.

  6. Click the Folder Icon and set the path to where you saved the extended asset library. Navigate to the folder that contains the materials and decorations folders, then click the Accept button at the bottom right of the File View dialog.

  7. Once the path is set click the Refresh button.

  8. The Assets will show in the asset shelf at the bottom of the interface. If the interface doesn't appear, click the arrow button at the bottom right of the interface.

  9. If you click the catalog selector icon. You can enable the tabs you want to appear in the asset shelf.

  10. Drag and drop the assets into the 3D viewport.

docs.title: Colors aren't displaying correctly
docs.text: If you see a warning at the top of the sidebar about "Object Color Type is not enabled", click Open Recommended Settings. Home Builder requires the viewport shading color type to be set to Object for colors to display correctly.

docs.title: Add-on doesn't appear after installation
docs.text:
• Make sure you're using Blender 5.0 or later
  • Check Window → Toggle System Console for error messages
  • Try reinstalling the add-on

docs.title: Cabinets aren't snapping to walls
docs.text:
• Make sure the wall was created with Home Builder's Draw Walls command (not a manually created mesh)
  • Check that you're clicking along a wall surface during placement
